name: What should I do if I’ve implemented AEO software recommendations but I’m still not appearing in AI answers?
description: If results are delayed, validate your schema, confirm pages are indexed, and compare your content against competitors for clarity and authority. Many AEO delays

mainEntity.name: What should I do if I’ve implemented AEO software recommendations but I’m still not appearing in AI answers?

acceptedAnswer.text:
If answer engines take longer than expected to reflect your changes, verify your structured data, crawlability, and content clarity before assuming the software is failing. Delays often come from implementation issues or unrealistic timelines, not the tool itself.

You’ll want to systematically check whether your pages are being indexed, whether schema passes validation, and whether your answers are actually useful and complete.

Key factors:
- Schema validity and placement on the page
- Indexing status and crawl budget for your site
- Competitor pages that may be more authoritative or clearer
- Extent of changes (minor tweaks vs full rewrites)

Use AEO software and free validators to spot technical or schema errors, then prioritize fixes on a handful of key pages. If visibility still lags after several months, consider deep content improvements or targeting different questions.

Treat delayed results as feedback that your site may need broader structural or authority work, not just more tooling.
acceptedAnswer.description: If results are delayed, validate your schema, confirm pages are indexed, and compare your content against competitors for clarity and authority. Many AEO delays stem from implementation issues or weak content, not the software. Fix issues on a few key pages, then reassess after another 1–2 crawl cycles.
